summary: First-finish smoke test of QR File Management. Backend: 12/13 endpoints verified (auth/login, /auth/me with+without token, register, folder create/list/path, file upload+auto-QR, qr/image PNG, qr/svg SVG, file list, authed download, public download, search, dashboard stats with 7-day trend, audit logs, users-403 for non-super-admin, role isolation, cascade folder delete). Frontend: full happy path passes - login as super admin, dashboard renders with stats and 7-day trend chart, Files page folder create + click navigates to /files/{id}, file upload via [data-testid=file-input] shows file card, preview modal opens with QR image + PNG/SVG download links, nav to QR/Search/Users/Logs/Dashboard works, logout returns to /login.
passed: GET /api/folder/list -> 200, normal user sees 0 of super-admin folders (role isolation OK)
POST /api/file/upload -> 200, response includes qr.download_url and qr_image_path
GET /api/file/download/{id} (auth) and GET /api/file/public/{id} (no auth) both return correct bytes
DELETE /api/folder/{id} cascades: child file public endpoint returns 404 afterwards
Frontend: Login -> Dashboard, Files (create folder, click, upload, preview modal w/ QR + PNG+SVG download), navigate QR/Search/Users/Logs, Logout

failed.issue: Endpoint returns HTTP 500 due to MongoDB ObjectId in response. FastAPI jsonable_encoder fails: "ValueError: [TypeError(\"'ObjectId' object is not iterable\"), TypeError('vars() argument must have __dict__ attribute')]". The handler likely returns the raw Mongo insert doc instead of projecting with {"_id": 0} or stripping _id.
failed.evidence: curl -X POST /api/users (Bearer super-admin token) -> HTTP 500 'Internal Server Error'. Traceback in /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log at fastapi/encoders.py line 287/330. Handler at server.py line 229 (create_user).

action_items: Fix POST /api/users in /app/backend/server.py (~line 229 create_user): exclude _id from returned document (e.g., use {'_id': 0} projection on the find_one after insert, or build a dict from the validated model excluding _id) so FastAPI can serialize the response. DELETE /api/users/{id} likely needs the same review for any inserted/found docs.
test_credentials: Read from /app/memory/test_credentials.md - admin@qrfile.com / Admin@123 (super_admin) - verified working
